turns.user_message: I just sat at my desk for a solid twenty minutes staring at a blinking cursor, completely unable to type a single word. It feels like my brain has just hit a wall that I cannot climb over no matter how hard I try. I keep telling myself that I just need a quick break to reset, but the heavy fog in my head refuses to lift. This has been creeping up on me for months, turning simple tasks into these massive, exhausting mountains. I really just want to feel like myself again instead of dragging through every single hour.

That exact moment of staring at the screen has become my absolute normal lately, and I catch myself freezing up over the smallest things every single day. I used to power through these slumps with a little extra focus, but now my mind just completely shuts down the moment I sit down to start. It is incredibly frustrating to watch the hours slip by while I sit there doing absolutely nothing productive. I feel like I am constantly running on empty, trying to force a machine that has completely run out of gas. The guilt piles up every evening, making it even harder to face the next morning.

I honestly do not know how much longer I can keep pretending that I am fine while this complete mental shutdown takes over my daily routine. Every time I try to push past that familiar wall and finally look at the glowing screen, my chest gets tight and my thoughts just scatter into a million useless fragments. I am so tired of forcing myself to care about things that used to matter, only to end up feeling completely hollow inside. It feels like I am trapped in a thick, invisible loop that drains every last drop of energy I have left. I just want the constant pressure to stop so I can finally breathe again.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by referencing a specific recent moment. This has been going on for a while.
